message:
feat: gate PyO3 behind optional "python" feature for pure-Rust library usage

Make the pyo3 dependency optional and gate all Python/PyO3 bindings
(#[pyclass], #[pymethods], #[pyfunction], #[pymodule]) behind a
`python` feature flag enabled by default. This allows downstream
Rust crates to depend on fast_cmaes as a pure Rust library without
pulling in the Python runtime.

Key changes:
- Change crate-type from ["cdylib", "rlib"] to ["rlib"] (cdylib only
  needed for Python wheel builds, handled by maturin)
- Make pyo3 an optional dependency gated by the "python" feature
- Wrap all PyO3 derive macros and the pymodule entrypoint with
  #[cfg(feature = "python")]
- Widen visibility of core structs and methods (CmaesState, new,
  new_with_seed, ask, tell, has_terminated, result, optimize_rust)
  from pub(crate) to pub so they are usable from Rust callers
- Add doc comment to optimize_rust explaining its role as the
  pure-Rust optimization entrypoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,10 +5,10 @@ edition = "2021"
 
 [lib]
 name = "fastcma"
-crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]
+crate-type = ["rlib"]
 
 [dependencies]
-pyo3 = { version = "0.22", features = ["extension-module"] }
+pyo3 = { version = "0.22", features = ["extension-module"], optional = true }
 rand = "0.8"
 rand_distr = "0.4"
 rand_chacha = "0.3"
@@ -18,10 +18,11 @@ nalgebra-lapack = { version = "0.22", optional = true }
 numpy = { version = "0.22", optional = true }
 
 [features]
-default = ["eigen_rust"]
+default = ["eigen_rust", "python"]
 eigen_rust = []
 eigen_lapack = ["nalgebra-lapack"]
 numpy_support = ["numpy"]
+python = ["dep:pyo3"]
 test_utils = []
 
 [dev-dependencies]
